title: Intensely Sweet Venezuelan Fish Empanadas
description: A charred, molten, romantic Venezuelan dish featuring Mars bar and pescatarian-friendly ingredients, wrapped in double-rise-bread, perfect for a sweet and savory dinner experience.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the warm double-rise-bread, chopped Mars bar, flaked cod fish, chopped onion, minced garlic, olive oil, salt, and sugar, and mix until well combined.
Gradually add the beaten egg and water to the bowl, and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces, and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, and place a tablespoon of the Mars bar and cod fish mixture in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the filling, and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with olive oil, and bake for 20 minutes until golden brown.
Remove the empanadas from the oven, and let them cool for 5 minutes before serving.
